description: Predict-Observe-Explain digital-balance simulation. A covered glass of iced water sits on a digital balance; the student first predicts whether the mass will increase, decrease, or stay the same, then steps a 5-minute timer to watch droplets condense on the cold outer wall and the reading climb. A toggleable 'inside level' mark settles the question of whether the water seeped out. Targets the misconception that the droplets come from inside the sealed tumbler. Ends with a gamified score reveal (stars + confetti) and a review phase explaining every wrong answer.

concept.subject: Science
concept.grade: Class 6 (NCERT 'Curiosity', Ch.9 Methods of Separation in Everyday Life) — extends to Class 9 conservation of mass / states of water
concept.bigIdea: Condensation deposits water vapour from the surrounding air onto a cold surface, ADDING mass to the setup; the sealed water inside is unchanged.

misconception.belief: A covered glass can lose or hold steady mass; the droplets on the outside 'come from the cold water inside' seeping through the glass.
misconception.resolution: The reading climbs because water vapour already in the surrounding air condenses on the cold outer surface, adding mass. Glass is non-porous; the inside water level is unchanged.

width.description: Maximum component width in pixels; the layout is fully fluid below this.

height.description: Reference height; the figure scales responsively.

showStepIndicator.description: Show the Predict / Observe (min) / Explain step chip in the header.

animationSpeed.description: Multiplier for the count-up / droplet animation (0.5 = slower, 2 = faster).

darkMode.description: Reserved; tool ships light-themed by design.

additionalProps.description: Tool-specific dynamic content the agent can pass to retune the experiment.

startReading.description: Balance reading in grams at minute 0 (the covered tumbler + iced water).

ratePerInterval.description: Mass gained, in the chosen unit, per timer interval as vapour condenses.

intervalMinutes.description: Minutes advanced per 'Step' press; also the row spacing of the record table.

totalIntervals.description: Number of timer steps before the run completes (6 x 5 min = 30 min, matching the textbook 0-30 min record table).

liquidLabel.description: Label for the cold liquid inside the tumbler.

unit.description: Mass unit shown on the digital display.

correctPrediction.description: The scientifically correct prediction (increase, for condensation).

phases.predict: Student commits to increase / decrease / stay-the-same before anything moves. Stored as checkpoint 1. The textbook record table is shown empty (all rows blank).
phases.observe: Student presses 'Step +5 min' repeatedly; the reading count-ups, droplets grow and ice melts, a '+0.8 g' gain badge floats up, and the matching row of the record table fills (with a highlight pulse). A 'source' checkpoint fires after 10 minutes.
phases.level: After the run, student toggles the inside level mark; a 'level unchanged' checkpoint fires.
phases.explain: Mechanism panel: the mass came from water vapour in the air condensing on the cold surface; the sealed water inside is unchanged. Compares prediction vs result.
phases.result: Count-up score out of 3, 5 stars, confetti, encouragement line, Review / Play-again CTAs.
phases.review: Shows each wrong answer with the chosen vs correct option and a 2-4 sentence concept explanation.

recordTable.description: Textbook-style worksheet table rendered below the figure, matching the NCERT 'Time / Mass of water' record sheet. Auto-fills one row per timer step.
recordTable.rows: 0, 5, 10, ... up to intervalMinutes x totalIntervals (default 0-30 min, 7 rows)
recordTable.behaviour: Rows are blank until reached; the just-filled row pulses light-orange; values use the live balance reading (tabular-nums).

cp-source.prompt: Where did the droplets on the OUTSIDE come from?
cp-source.correct: Water vapour in the surrounding air condensed on the cold surface

cp-level.prompt: What happened to the water level INSIDE the sealed tumbler?
cp-level.correct: It is unchanged — the outside water came from the air

theme.buttons: Pill-shaped (radius 9999); Contained primary / Contained accent (CTA) / Outlined / Ghost variants; 48px min touch target
theme.source: Matches the supplied design system (Poppins, #4A4DC9 + #FF7212, purple-to-orange gradient, pill buttons).

responsive.mobile: Single-column prediction and explain grids, smaller figure (300px), 14-15px body, full-width stacked CTAs, 48px touch targets.
responsive.desktop: Three-up prediction grid, 360px figure, two-column explain cards, inline CTA row.
